title: Data Quality
description: Metric—such as a number, graph, uncertainty budget, or flag—that enables users to evaluate the fitness for purpose of data or derived products.

definitions.definition: Metric—such as a number, graph, uncertainty budget, or flag—that enables users to evaluate the fitness for purpose of data or derived products.
definitions.notes: Quality Information: Secondary data required to assess the primary data set’s fitness for purpose, e.g. calibration and validation data and quality control results.
definitions.sources:
- [CEOS EO Data Stewardship Glossary](https://ceos.org/document_management/Working_Groups/WGISS/Interest_Groups/Data_Stewardship/White_Papers/EO-DataStewardshipGlossary.pdf)
- NESDIS Data Management Lexicon and Related Terms
